title: Help Birds by Not Feeding Them in Summer
summary: Feeding birds in your garden can be harmful during warmer months, says the RSPB, a bird charity. From May to October, bird feeders can spread a disease called trichomonosis, which makes birds sick and can stop them from eating or breathing. This disease has caused greenfinches and chaffinches to become endangered, with greenfinch numbers dropping a lot. The RSPB advises people to take a break from feeding birds in summer to keep them safe. Instead, you can offer small amounts of food like mealworms or fat balls only for a day or two, and always clean feeders weekly. Also, move feeders to new spots and change water daily to prevent germs. By following these tips, you can help protect garden birds and slow the disease spread.

What disease spreads at bird feeders in warmer months?
Which bird has become endangered because of this disease?
How often should bird feeders be cleaned?
What can people offer birds in small amounts all year?
Why is it important to move feeders after cleaning?

background_read: Birds are animals that fly and live in gardens, parks, and forests. People often feed birds to help them, especially in winter when food is scarce. However, in summer, feeding can cause problems because germs from sick birds can spread at feeders. The RSPB is a group that cares for birds and gives advice to keep them healthy. By learning about bird diseases and how to feed safely, you can enjoy watching birds without harming them.
Article_Structure: WHO: The RSPB, a bird charity, and people who feed garden birds.
WHAT: Advising not to feed birds in summer to prevent disease spread, and giving tips for safe feeding.
WHERE: In gardens across the UK where bird feeders are used.
WHY: To protect birds like greenfinches from trichomonosis and help slow the disease.

perspectives.description: The RSPB wants to save birds by reducing disease risks through seasonal feeding.
